name: OlympusDAO
report: The redeem function in BondFixedExpiryTeller lacks sufficient validation on the input token_ parameter. Since token_ is externally controllable and not restricted to legitimate bond tokens, an attacker deployed a malicious custom contract that mimics a valid ERC20BondToken. When redeem was called with this fake token, the function burned tokens from the caller (which had no real effect) but then transferred amount_ of the underlying asset—configured by the attacker to return OHM—from the teller contract. This allowed the attacker to drain approximately 30,000 OHM (valued at $300K) from the BondFixedExpiryTeller.
